mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses plant pollination mechanisms including wind, water, and animal-mediated transfer, and the co-evolutionary adaptations of flower morphology to attract pollinators—central to the preparatory events of plant development outlined in C.2.b.

The cell as the fundamental unit of life underpins all developmental processes; the episode explicitly covers cell division (growth, B.1), the enormous variety of differentiated cell types (B.3), and cellular signaling processes that integrate and control development (B.4).

The cloning of Dolly the sheep demonstrated that differentiated adult cells can be reprogrammed to develop into a complete organism, directly relevant to differentiation (B.3), developmental control (B.4), and development in vitro (F.5).

Mutations as errors in DNA replication are a direct cause of developmental malformations (F.3) and are linked to aging and cancer (E.2, causes of aging), connecting molecular-level events to disrupted developmental outcomes.
Mitochondrial dysfunction is directly implicated in cellular aging and neurodegenerative disease, providing a mechanistic explanation for senescence in mammals (E.1) and causes of aging (E.2).
